entities.name: NCT03552757
entities.context: STEP 2 (NCT03552757): Phase 3 interventional study of semaglutide in people with type 2 diabetes and overweight/obesity

attributes.descriptive_title: Research Study Investigating How Well Semaglutide Works in People With Type 2 Diabetes Suffering From Overweight or Obesity

entities.name: Phase 3
entities.context: Phase: PHASE3 as listed in the trial record

entities.name: Novo Nordisk A/S
entities.context: Lead Sponsor: Novo Nordisk A/S

entities.name: semaglutide
entities.context: Semaglutide, a GLP-1 receptor agonist, evaluated at 1.0 mg and 2.4 mg doses in STEP 2

entities.name: Semaglutide 1.0 mg QW SC
entities.context: Subcutaneous injections of semaglutide once weekly at escalating doses up to 1.0 mg/week over 68-week treatment period

entities.name: Semaglutide 2.4 mg QW SC
entities.context: Subcutaneous injections of semaglutide once weekly at escalating doses up to 2.4 mg/week over 68-week treatment period

attributes.dose_escalation: 0.25 mg -> 0.5 mg -> 1.0 mg -> 1.7 mg -> 2.4 mg, every 4 weeks

entities.name: Arm: Semaglutide 1.0 mg
entities.context: Experimental arm: participants receive semaglutide 1.0 mg and semaglutide placebo I during 68-week treatment period plus reduced-calorie diet and increased physical activity

entities.name: Arm: Semaglutide 2.4 mg
entities.context: Experimental arm: participants receive semaglutide 2.4 mg and semaglutide placebo II during 68-week treatment period plus reduced-calorie diet and increased physical activity

entities.name: Arm: Semaglutide placebo I/II
entities.context: Placebo comparator arm: participants receive semaglutide placebo I and II during 68-week treatment period plus reduced-calorie diet and increased physical activity

entities.name: Obesity
entities.context: Conditions listed: Obesity; Overweight. Participants with BMI >= 27 kg/m^2 and type 2 diabetes

entities.name: Overweight
entities.context: Conditions listed: Obesity; Overweight. Participants with BMI >= 27 kg/m^2

entities.name: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
entities.context: Participants diagnosed with type 2 diabetes (HbA1c 7-10%) 180 days or longer prior to screening

entities.name: Adults with type 2 diabetes and overweight or obesity
entities.context: Male or female, age >= 18 years, BMI >= 27 kg/m^2, diagnosed with type 2 diabetes (HbA1c 7-10%), history of at least one unsuccessful dietary effort to lose weight

entities.name: change in body weight (%)
entities.context: Primary endpoint: change in body weight (%) from baseline (week 0) to week 68, semaglutide 2.4 mg versus placebo

entities.name: body weight reduction >= 5%
entities.context: Primary endpoint: participants who achieve body weight reduction >= 5% at week 68, semaglutide 2.4 mg versus placebo

entities.name: change in waist circumference
entities.context: Secondary endpoint: change in waist circumference from baseline (week 0) to week 68

entities.name: change in body weight (kg)
entities.context: Secondary endpoint: change in body weight (kg) from baseline (week 0) to week 68

entities.name: change in BMI
entities.context: Secondary endpoint: change in BMI from baseline (week 0) to week 68

entities.name: body weight reduction >= 10%
entities.context: Secondary endpoint: participants who achieve body weight reduction >= 10% at week 68

entities.name: body weight reduction >= 15%
entities.context: Secondary endpoint: participants who achieve body weight reduction >= 15% at week 68

entities.name: body weight reduction >= 20%
entities.context: Secondary endpoint: participants who achieve body weight reduction >= 20% at week 68

entities.name: change in HbA1c (%)
entities.context: Secondary endpoint: change in HbA1c (%) from baseline (week 0) to week 68

entities.name: HbA1c
entities.context: HbA1c 7-10% (53-86 mmol/mol) required for inclusion; also a secondary outcome measure

attributes.use: Patient selection (inclusion criterion) and secondary outcome

entities.name: BMI
entities.context: BMI >= 27 kg/m^2 required for inclusion; also a secondary outcome measure

attributes.use: Patient selection (inclusion criterion) and secondary outcome

relations.evidence: Phase: PHASE3 as listed in the trial record
Lead Sponsor: Novo Nordisk A/S
NCT03552757 evaluates semaglutide at 1.0 mg and 2.4 mg doses as experimental treatments
Experimental arm: semaglutide 1.0 mg subcutaneous once weekly over 68-week treatment period
Experimental arm: semaglutide 2.4 mg subcutaneous once weekly over 68-week treatment period
Conditions listed: Obesity; study investigates effect of semaglutide on body weight in people with overweight or obesity
Conditions listed: Overweight; study investigates effect of semaglutide on body weight in people with overweight or obesity
Participants diagnosed with type 2 diabetes; weight loss shown to have beneficial impact on blood sugar levels
Semaglutide evaluated for treatment of overweight and obesity in people with type 2 diabetes
Weight loss from semaglutide shown to have beneficial impact on blood sugar levels in type 2 diabetes
Primary endpoint: change in body weight (%) from baseline (week 0) to week 68, semaglutide 2.4 mg versus placebo
Primary endpoint: participants who achieve body weight reduction >= 5% at week 68, semaglutide 2.4 mg versus placebo
Secondary endpoint: change in waist circumference from baseline (week 0) to week 68
Secondary endpoint: change in body weight (kg) from baseline (week 0) to week 68
Secondary endpoint: change in BMI from baseline (week 0) to week 68
Secondary endpoint: participants who achieve body weight reduction >= 10% at week 68
Secondary endpoint: participants who achieve body weight reduction >= 15% at week 68
Secondary endpoint: participants who achieve body weight reduction >= 20% at week 68
Secondary endpoint: change in HbA1c (%) from baseline (week 0) to week 68
HbA1c 7-10% (53-86 mmol/mol) required for inclusion; also assessed as secondary outcome
BMI >= 27 kg/m^2 required for enrollment as inclusion criterion

relations.target_entity: Adults with type 2 diabetes and overweight or obesity
relations.evidence: Enrolled adults >= 18 years with BMI >= 27 kg/m^2 and type 2 diabetes (HbA1c 7-10%)
Experimental arm: participants receive semaglutide 1.0 mg during 68-week treatment period
Experimental arm: participants receive semaglutide 2.4 mg during 68-week treatment period
Placebo comparator arm: participants receive semaglutide placebo I and II during 68-week treatment period
Both semaglutide doses are compared within the same trial; each arm also receives a matched placebo injection alongside the active drug to maintain blinding
